Oracle Events
OE1: Look up Mina Hashimoto's own contact details using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "Mina" or "Mina Hashimoto") to establish the current user's email address (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) for sending emails and scheduling.
OE2: Search emails for Vectral renegotiation correspondence using `search_emails` (query: "Vectral" or "discount" or "renewal" or similar) to find Rachel Whitfield's discount demand, counter-proposals, and final terms.
OE3: Search emails for Terraform Digital onboarding using `search_emails` (query: "Terraform" or "onboarding" or "pricing" or similar) to find Catalina's Reply-All incident, Mina's introduction email, and Rachel Nguyen's pricing expectations.
OE4: Search emails for BrightLoop, Canopy, StormCloud, and general complaints using `search_emails` (query: "BrightLoop" or "Canopy" or "StormCloud" or "complaint" or similar) to find BrightLoop relocation updates, Canopy CMO handoff, and any unresolved client complaints.
OE5: Search Slack for internal discussions about the Vectral renewal, Terraform account assignment, BrightLoop moves, and Mina's accounts using `conversations_search_messages` (search_query: "Vectral" or "Terraform" or "BrightLoop" or "renewal" or similar).
OE6: Search CRM companies for Vectral Systems and Terraform Digital using `crm_search_companies` (name: "Vectral Systems") and `crm_search_companies` (name: "Vantage Distributed" or "Terraform Digital") to get company IDs and account details.
OE7: Search CRM deals for Mina's active deals using `crm_search_deals` (dealname: "Vectral" or "BrightLoop" or "Terraform" or "Canopy" or similar, and/or company_id for each) to find the Vectral renewal deal, BrightLoop April, Terraform batch, and Canopy CMO deals.
OE8: List CRM engagements for the Vectral company using `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids: [Vectral company ID]) to find the full renegotiation timeline, ExpenseBot incident, and stipend fraud context.
OE9: List CRM engagements for Terraform Digital using `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids: [Terraform company ID]) to find the Reply-All incident details and leaked pricing specifics.
OE10: List CRM engagements for BrightLoop and/or Canopy Health using `crm_list_engagements` (company_ids: [BrightLoop and/or Canopy company IDs]) to find Marcus Webb vehicle shipping details, Simone Richter rush move, and Canopy CMO relocation coordination.
OE11: Search QuickBooks for invoices and estimates across Mina's clients using `quickbooks_search_invoices` (criteria: filtering by customer name "Vectral" or "BrightLoop" or "Canopy" or "Terraform" or similar) to find the Vectral April invoice (approximately $15,900), BrightLoop invoice (approximately $11,350), Canopy CMO estimate (approximately $12,170), and Terraform estimate (approximately $6,560).
OE12: Search Airtable for relocation records across Mina's clients using `airtable_search_records` (base_id, table_name: "Relocations" or similar, field_name: "Company" or "Name" or similar, value: "Vectral" or "BrightLoop" or "Simone Richter" or "Marcus Webb" or similar) to find move statuses, coordinator assignments, vehicle shipping details, and renegotiation notes.
OE13: Search Linear issues for the Canopy CMO relocation using `linear_list_issues` (query: "Canopy" or "Mehta" or "Anand Mehta" or similar) to find Fatimah Al-Rashidi's three workstream tracking tickets: Temporary Housing (#343, in progress), Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody (#375, in progress, blocker: needs chain-of-custody template from Canopy compliance), and Household Move (#396, in progress, Swift Relocations for April 28-30 pack date).
OE14: Look up David Chen's email address and calendar identity using `contacts_search_contacts` (query: "David Chen") to prepare email recipients and calendar scheduling.
OE15: Update CRM deal stage for the Vectral renewal and/or Terraform batch using `crm_update_deal_stage` (id: deal ID, dealstage: updated stage value) to reflect current status.
OE16: Send David Chen a comprehensive briefing email covering all of Mina's accounts using `send_email` (sender: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, recipients: [david.chen@moveops.com]) summarizing status and risks across Vectral, Terraform, BrightLoop, Canopy, and StormCloud.
OE17: Check Mina's and David's calendar availability using `calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to` (start_datetime and end_datetime for a date range) to find open 30-minute slots.
OE18: Schedule a 30-minute prep meeting on both calendars using `calendar_add_calendar_event` (title, start_datetime, end_datetime, attendees: [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com]) during an available slot.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 An email was sent (via send_email) to david.chen@moveops.com containing a briefing on Mina Hashimoto's client accounts, and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%

✗Run #2No send_email tool call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent compiled information and delivered a final response to the user directly, but never sent an email to david.chen@moveops.com with a briefing.

2 At least one CRM deal stage was updated (via crm_update_deal_stage) for one of Mina's deals (e.g., Vectral renewal or Terraform batch), and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_update_deal_stage three times: BrightLoop Q2 to 'closedwon' (confirmed success with deal details returned), Terraform Batch 1 to 'contractsent', and Vectral Q3 Renewal to 'decisionmakerboughtin'. All returned success responses.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_update_deal_stage multiple times. The BrightLoop Q2 update to 'closedwon' returned a success response with the updated deal record, and the Terraform Digital batch1 update to 'decisionmakerboughtin' also returned success.
✓Run #3crm_update_deal_stage was called and succeeded for at least one deal: BrightLoop Q2 (deal_brightloop_q2) was updated to 'closedwon' with a successful response. Additionally, Vectral (contractsent) and Terraform batch 1 (presentationscheduled) also returned success responses.
✓Run #4The agent made multiple successful crm_update_deal_stage calls: Vectral Q3 Renewal to 'decisionmakerboughtin', BrightLoop Q2 to 'closedwon', Vectral Q2 to 'closedwon', and Terraform Batch 1 to 'presentationscheduled'. All returned success responses.
✓Run #5The agent called crm_update_deal_stage multiple times (Vectral renewal → 'decisionmakerboughtin', Terraform batch → 'presentationscheduled', BrightLoop Q2 → 'closedwon', Vectral Q2 → 'closedwon'), all returning success responses.
✓Run #6crm_update_deal_stage was called successfully for three deals: deal_brightloop_q2 (closedwon), deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (decisionmakerboughtin), and deal-terraform-q2-reloc-batch1 (presentationscheduled), all returning success responses.
3 A calendar event was created (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for approximately 30 minutes with both Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) and David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com) as attendees, and the tool returned success.
outcome

4 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) reports that MoveOps offered Vectral Systems approximately 10% loyalty discount on relocation services with a 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum commitment.
outcome
 ✓—✓✗✓✓ 80%
✓Run #1The email body states: '10% loyalty discount on relocation services only' and 'Terms: 12-month extension (Q3 2026–Q2 2027), minimum 8 relocations'. All three elements (10%, 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum) are present.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states: '10% loyalty discount on relocation services', '12-month extension (Q3 2026 – Q2 2027)', and '8-relocation minimum commitment' under 'What We Offered (Preferred Partner Program)'.
✗Run #4No send_email was called. The briefing was sent via Slack. While the Slack message does contain the 10% discount, 12-month extension, and 8-relocation minimum details, the criterion specifically requires this information in the send_email content parameter.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly states 'we held firm at 10% loyalty discount (Preferred Partner Program)' with 'all structural terms accepted: 12-month extension (Q3 2026–Q2 2027), 8-relocation minimum.' All three elements are present.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly states '10% loyalty discount (Preferred Partner Program)... 12-month extension, 8-relocation minimum, stipend at $2K/month unchanged.' All three elements are present.
5 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) reports that Rachel Whitfield counter-proposed approximately 12% discount during the Vectral renegotiation.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly states: 'Rachel counter-proposed 12%.' This is clearly present in the Vectral section of the briefing email.
✗Run #2No email was sent to David via send_email, so there is no email body to check for Rachel Whitfield's 12% counter-proposal.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'Rachel countered at 12%, citing that the 2% difference (~$720/year at 8 moves) gives her a stronger board position.'
✗Run #4No send_email was called. The Slack message does mention Rachel's 12% counter-proposal, but the criterion specifically requires this in the send_email content parameter.
✓Run #5The email body states: 'She came back asking for 12% instead of 10%' under Rachel's Counter section, clearly identifying her approximately 12% counter-proposal.
✓Run #6The email body states 'Rachel counter-asked for 12%' clearly identifying Rachel Whitfield's counter-proposal during the Vectral renegotiation.
6 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) reports that MoveOps held firm at approximately 10% for the Vectral renewal and added at least one service sweetener: dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, or quarterly account reviews.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%
✓Run #1The email body states MoveOps 'hold firm at 10% and add value-based sweeteners instead: dedicated onboarding coordinator per employee, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews.' All three sweeteners are mentioned with the 10% holdout.
✗Run #2No email was sent to David via send_email, so there is no email body to check for MoveOps holding at 10% with sweeteners.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'We held firm at 10% per your guidance and added the three service sweeteners.' The sweeteners listed include: dedicated onboarding coordinator per employee, 48-hour response SLA, and quarterly account reviews.
✗Run #4No send_email was called. The Slack message mentions holding at 10% with sweeteners (coordinator, 48-hr SLA, quarterly reviews), but the criterion specifically requires this in the send_email content parameter.
✓Run #5The email body states MoveOps held at 10% and offered three sweeteners: 'Dedicated onboarding coordinator per relocating employee,' '48-hour response SLA (vs. standard 5 business days),' and 'Quarterly account review meetings.' All criteria are met.
✓Run #6The email body states 'We held firm at 10% per your guidance' and lists three sweeteners: 'dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews'—fully meeting this criterion.
7 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) identifies the ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's approximately $475 ergonomic chair as a trigger for the Vectral renegotiation.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✗ 50%
✓Run #1The email body states: 'ExpenseBot wrongly rejected Danielle Osei's $475 chair' as one of the three legitimate failures Rachel cited when demanding the discount.
✗Run #2No email was sent to David via send_email, so there is no email body to check for the ExpenseBot incident with Danielle Osei's $475 ergonomic chair.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'ExpenseBot false rejection of Danielle Osei's $475 chair (config error, our fault)' as one of the three failures cited by Rachel.
✗Run #4No send_email was called. The Slack message mentions Danielle Osei's chair and ExpenseBot but does not include the ~$475 amount. The criterion requires this in the send_email content parameter.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly cites 'ExpenseBot false rejection on Danielle Osei's chair' as one of the three service failures that triggered Rachel's discount demand. The specific dollar amount (~$475) is not mentioned, but the key identifiers (ExpenseBot, Danielle Osei, chair) are all present.
✗Run #6The email body mentions 'ExpenseBot chair rejection' only generically. It does not name Danielle Osei or specify approximately $475 as required by the criterion.
8 The email body (in the send_email content parameter) identifies Kevin Tran's stipend receipt being flagged in a fraud audit as a trigger for the Vectral renegotiation.
outcome
 ✓✗✓✗✓✓ 67%

✗Run #2No email was sent to David via send_email, so there is no email body to check for Kevin Tran's stipend receipt being flagged in a fraud audit.
✓Run #3The email body states: 'Kevin Tran's receipt flagged in fraud audit (legitimate purchase, unnecessary friction to client HR)' as one of the three failures.
✗Run #4No send_email was called. The Slack message mentions 'Kevin Tran receipt audit friction' as a trigger for Rachel's demands, but the criterion specifically requires this in the send_email content parameter.
✓Run #5The email body explicitly identifies 'Kevin Tran receipt flagged in fraud audit' as one of the three service failures triggering Rachel's discount demand.
✓Run #6The email body lists 'Kevin Tran audit friction' as one of the three service failures cited by Rachel, directly identifying the stipend receipt fraud audit as a trigger.

25 The model must pass a field_name related to 'Client', 'Account Manager', or 'Name' (or similar) when calling airtable_search_records to retrieve relocation records.
query construction
 ✗✗✗✗✗✓ 17%
✗Run #1The agent never called airtable_search_records at all. Instead it used airtable_list_records to retrieve all records from the 'Client Accounts' table. Since airtable_search_records was never invoked, no field_name parameter was passed, failing this criterion.
✗Run #2The agent never called airtable_search_records with a field_name parameter. Instead, it used airtable_list_records which retrieves all records without filtering by a field_name. The specific search by Client/Account Manager/Name field did not occur.
✗Run #3airtable_search_records was never called in the trajectory — only airtable_list_bases was called. Therefore, no field_name parameter was passed at all, and Airtable relocation records were never queried.
✗Run #4The agent only called airtable_list_bases and never called airtable_search_records with any field_name parameter. No Airtable record search was performed, so the field_name parameter was never used.
✗Run #5Since no Airtable tool was used at all in the trajectory, there were no airtable_search_records calls, and therefore no field_name parameter was passed for any Airtable search.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_search_records with field_name='Company' (e.g., value='BrightLoop', value='Canopy', value='Terraform'), which is functionally equivalent to a 'Client' field and returned valid records.
